discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tone with tech/semis leading (SMH +1.5%, QQQ +1.25%) while defense (ITA -1.86%) lags despite Iran tensions and oil spike, suggesting market is reading ceasefire extension as de-escalation. Notable idiosyncratic movers: CALX crashed -17% on apparent earnings miss, BBY down -4.5% on CEO transition news that looks like an overreaction given the 'succession' framing, MANH jumping despite below-average volume.

notable_index_moves: SMH +1.5% and XLK +1.37% leading; AVGO +2.76%, ORCL +2.98% suggesting renewed AI infra bid despite Google TPU announcement
ITA -1.86% is notable divergence from risk-on tape; suggests market pricing in Iran ceasefire/peace talks reducing defense demand
VIX -1.7% to 19.16 confirms risk-on despite $100 oil and tanker seizures
XLE only +0.92% despite oil at $100 — underwhelming energy response worth noting

discoveries.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
discoveries.mechanism: Knee-jerk reaction to CEO transition announcement (Corie Barry stepping down late October, replaced by internal merchandising chief Jason Bonfig). Internal successor + 6-month runway typically does not warrant -4.5% in a risk-on tape. Low volume (0.47x) suggests no conviction selling — looks like algos reacting to headline.
discoveries.catalyst: Best Buy names Jason Bonfig as new CEO, replacing Corie Barry in late October
research_pointers: Check if any guidance cut or strategic shift was bundled with the CEO announcement
Review Bonfig's background — internal merchandising head suggests continuity, not disruption
Compare to prior retailer CEO transitions (e.g., orderly successions rarely produce sustained -5% moves)
Watch tariff exposure angle — BBY imports heavily from China; tariff news could be the real driver, not CEO
Check if analyst downgrades accompanied the announcement
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Evidence of concurrent guidance cut, strategy shift toward costly turnaround, or that Bonfig's appointment signals activist/board dissatisfaction with current trajectory

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: No news in provided feed explains a -17% move. Likely earnings/guidance miss given magnitude, but cannot confirm from data provided. Volume 1.85x is notably modest for a -17% gap, which could indicate thin float reaction rather than institutional capitulation — or simply early-session volume.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown from provided data — likely earnings release (check for Q1 2026 print)
research_pointers: Pull Calix Q1 2026 earnings release and call transcript
Check guidance for Q2 and full-year — broadband access equipment sector has been lumpy
Compare to peers like ADTN, CIEN for sector read-through
Review BEAD program funding commentary — Calix is heavily exposed to rural broadband buildouts
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Confirmation that guidance was raised or that the drop reflects a clean beat (would make this a buying opportunity); conversely, evidence of structural demand collapse or customer loss would validate the move

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.mechanism: +5% on BELOW-average volume (0.64x) after +10% five-day run is unusual. Could be continuation of post-earnings drift or squeeze, but lack of volume suggests no new catalyst — potentially short-covering in supply chain software name given 'SMBs overhaul supply chains' narrative.
discoveries.catalyst: Possible sympathy with tariff-driven supply chain software demand thesis (Yahoo piece on SMBs overhauling supply chains)
research_pointers: Check if MANH reported earnings in past week (5d +10% suggests yes)
Review supply chain software peer moves — DSGX, SPS Commerce, SNPS enterprise
Low volume rally can fade quickly; watch for distribution
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Confirmation of a specific catalyst (analyst upgrade, contract win) would reclassify as rational; continued low-volume drift higher is a warning sign

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Iran ceasefire extension + tanker seizures in Hormuz (mixed signal — peace talks continuing but real disruption persists, oil back to $100)

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy — defense ETF down -1.86% in risk-on tape suggests peace-talk positioning
customer — airlines facing fuel cost surge, UAL already cut 2026 forecast
subject of Trump admin rescue package talks — binary catalyst

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Google unveils dedicated TPU chips for AI training/inference, directly targeting Nvidia

secondary_opportunities.relationship: competitor — but NVDA only +0.73%, market shrugging off threat
supplier — AVGO designs Google's TPUs, hence +2.76%

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump railing against Supreme Court tariff decision that requires $159B in tariff refunds; signals continued commitment to tariff regime and frustration with judicial constraints
trump_signals.note: Backward-looking complaint about already-decided case, not a new policy signal. Retailers with China exposure (BBY especially) may face continued uncertainty as administration seeks alternative tariff mechanisms. This COULD be the real reason BBY is down -4.5%, not the CEO news — tariff-exposed consumer electronics retailer on a day Trump is re-litigating tariffs. Worth flagging to Michael as an alternative hypothesis for BBY weakness.

discovery.no_signals_note: Moderate signal day. Primary actionable pointer is BBY overreaction thesis, but note the Trump tariff post creates a competing narrative that could justify more of the move than the CEO transition alone. CALX needs earnings data that wasn't in the feed before any directional read is possible.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
ai_announcements.summary: Infosys-OpenAI partnership for software dev, legacy modernization, DevOps automation at enterprise clients.

ai_announcements.headline: Google deepens Thinking Machines Lab ties with multi-billion-dollar GB300 deal
ai_announcements.summary: Mira Murati's lab signs multi-B deal with Google Cloud on Nvidia GB300 chips.

ai_announcements.headline: Unauthorized group claims access to Anthropic's Mythos cyber tool; Altman calls it 'fear-based marketing'
ai_announcements.summary: Report alleges third-party access to Anthropic's offensive cybersecurity model. Anthropic disputes system impact. OpenAI's Altman publicly attacks the product's positioning. ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL - flagged for bias grading.

ai_announcements.headline: SpaceX working with Cursor, has option to buy at $60B
ai_announcements.summary: SpaceX/xAI-Cursor tie-up in developer tools market; article notes Cursor competes with Anthropic/OpenAI who also sell direct to devs.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The stocks that moved today (CALX -16.9%, MANH +5.1%, GME +4.7%, BBY -4.6%) do not have clear direct links to today's AI announcements. CALX's 17% drop is almost certainly an earnings/guidance event (broadband access equipment, not an AI-exposure story) — volume multiple 1.85x and identical 5-day move confirm single-event driver. MANH's move is likely earnings-related (supply chain software; would actually be AI-enabled beneficiary, not victim). BBY weakness is consumer discretionary, not AI-driven. GME is idiosyncratic retail-trader flow. No credible sympathy-selling or AI-misread trade identified in today's tape. Recommend no action; the AI news flow (Infosys/OpenAI, Google Maps AI, Mythos drama) is not the marginal driver for any listed mover.
